main_paper.raw: {"methods":["RFWave: Multi-band Rectified Flow for audio waveform reconstruction","Rectified Flow for efficient sampling (straight transport trajectory)","Frame-level (STFT frame) modeling instead of sample point-level","Multi-band strategy for concurrent subband generation","Use of ConvNeXtV2 backbone for efficient processing","Three enhanced loss functions: energy-balanced loss, overlap loss, STFT loss","Optimized sampling strategy based on trajectory straightness"],"problems":["High latency and slow generation speed in diffusion-based audio waveform reconstruction due to sample point-level operation and many sampling steps","High GPU memory usage and computational demands in existing models","Instability and mode collapse in GAN-based waveform reconstruction","Need for high-fidelity and efficient audio waveform reconstruction from Mel-spectrograms or discrete acoustic tokens"],"datasets":[],"metrics":["Inference speed (generation speed)","Number of sampling steps","Audio quality (subjective and/or objective, but specific metric not named)"],"results":[{"metric":"Inference speed","value":"Up to 160x real-time on NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4090 GPU"},{"metric":"Number of sampling steps","value":"10 sampling steps for high-quality audio"}],"novelty_claims":["First diffusion-based audio waveform reconstruction model to achieve GAN-like speed while maintaining diffusion model quality and stability","Operates at STFT frame level, not sample point level, significantly reducing memory and computation","Integrates Rectified Flow with three novel loss functions (energy-balanced, overlap, STFT loss) for high-quality reconstruction with few sampling steps","Employs a multi-band strategy and ConvNeXtV2 backbone for concurrent subband generation and speed","Introduces a new sampling time point selection technique based on Rectified Flow trajectory straightness to enhance sample quality"]}

methods: RFWave: Multi-band Rectified Flow for audio waveform reconstruction
Rectified Flow for efficient sampling (straight transport trajectory)
Frame-level (STFT frame) modeling instead of sample point-level
Multi-band strategy for concurrent subband generation
Use of ConvNeXtV2 backbone for efficient processing
Three enhanced loss functions: energy-balanced loss, overlap loss, STFT loss
Optimized sampling strategy based on trajectory straightness
problems: High latency and slow generation speed in diffusion-based audio waveform reconstruction due to sample point-level operation and many sampling steps
High GPU memory usage and computational demands in existing models
Instability and mode collapse in GAN-based waveform reconstruction
Need for high-fidelity and efficient audio waveform reconstruction from Mel-spectrograms or discrete acoustic tokens

results.value: Up to 160x real-time on NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4090 GPU
10 sampling steps for high-quality audio

novelty_claims: First diffusion-based audio waveform reconstruction model to achieve GAN-like speed while maintaining diffusion model quality and stability
Operates at STFT frame level, not sample point level, significantly reducing memory and computation
Integrates Rectified Flow with three novel loss functions (energy-balanced, overlap, STFT loss) for high-quality reconstruction with few sampling steps
Employs a multi-band strategy and ConvNeXtV2 backbone for concurrent subband generation and speed
Introduces a new sampling time point selection technique based on Rectified Flow trajectory straightness to enhance sample quality

novelty_claims: WaveRNN achieves state-of-the-art audio quality with a single-layer RNN, matching WaveNet but with much greater sampling efficiency.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Chunked Autoregressive GAN (CARGAN): a hybrid GAN-based model for conditional waveform synthesis that autoregressively generates large chunks of waveform samples per forward pass"],"problems":["Artifacts in GAN-based conditional waveform synthesis, especially during mel-spectrogram inversion","Inability of GAN-based models to accurately preserve pitch and periodicity in generated audio","Tradeoff between high-quality audio (autoregressive models) and fast generation (non-autoregressive models)"],"datasets":[],"metrics":["Pitch error","Training time","Generation speed","Subjective quality"],"results":[{"metric":"Pitch error reduction","value":"40-60% reduction compared to prior state-of-the-art GAN-based models"},{"metric":"Training time reduction","value":"58% reduction compared to prior state-of-the-art GAN-based models"},{"metric":"Generation speed","value":"Maintains fast generation suitable for real-time or interactive applications"},{"metric":"Subjective quality","value":"Maintains or improves subjective quality compared to prior GAN-based models"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes CARGAN, a hybrid GAN-based model that autoregressively generates large chunks of waveform samples, combining the inductive bias of autoregression with the speed of GANs","Demonstrates that pitch and periodicity artifacts in GAN-based models are due to lack of autoregressive inductive bias, and that chunked autoregression can mitigate these artifacts","Shows that the optimal chunk size for chunked autoregression is related to the causal receptive field of the generator","Provides a detailed analysis and documentation of common artifacts in GAN-based waveform synthesis and their causes"]}

novelty_claims: Proposes a new generative modeling approach (InterFlow) using stochastic interpolants for continuous-time normalizing flows
Introduces a simple quadratic loss for velocity field estimation, avoiding the need for backpropagation through ODE solvers
Separates the choice of interpolant path from the optimization objective, unlike conventional MLE-based flows
Demonstrates that the method can be used for both sampling and likelihood estimation at any point along the interpolant

methods: Score-based generative modeling via stochastic differential equations (SDEs)
Reverse-time SDE sampling using neural network-based score estimation
Predictor-Corrector (PC) samplers combining SDE solvers with score-based MCMC (e.g., Langevin MCMC, HMC)
Deterministic sampling via probability flow ODE for exact likelihood computation
Unified framework encapsulating SMLD and DDPM as special cases
Controllable generation for inverse problems (e.g., class-conditional generation, inpainting, colorization)
problems: How to generate data from noise using a principled, flexible, and unified generative modeling framework
How to improve sample quality and likelihood estimation in score-based generative models

novelty_claims: Proposes a unified framework for score-based generative modeling using SDEs, generalizing and connecting SMLD and DDPM approaches.
Introduces the use of reverse-time SDEs for generative modeling, with neural networks estimating time-dependent scores.
Develops Predictor-Corrector samplers that combine SDE solvers with score-based MCMC for improved sampling.
Derives a probability flow ODE for deterministic sampling and exact likelihood computation.
Enables controllable generation and solving inverse problems (e.g., inpainting, colorization) with a single unconditional model.
Achieves state-of-the-art results on CIFAR-10 for both sample quality and likelihood, and demonstrates high-fidelity 1024x1024 image generation from a score-based model for the first time.
